infobox.value: ← [2014](/wiki/2014_NRL_Grand_Final "2014 NRL Grand Final")  [2016](/wiki/2016_NRL_Grand_Final "2016 NRL Grand Final") → | |

was the conclusive and premiership-deciding game of the [2015 NRL season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season") and was played on Sunday 4 October at Sydney's [ANZ Stadium](/wiki/Stadium_Australia "Stadium Australia") between the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") and [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").[[1]](#cite_note-Australian_Broadcasting_Corporation_-_4_October_2015_-_NRL_Grand_Final:_North_Queensland_Cowboys_beat_Brisbane_Broncos_in_golden-point_thriller-1) North Queensland won the match 17–16 in [golden point](/wiki/Golden_point "Golden point") extra time, claiming their first [premiership title](/wiki/National_Rugby_League#Title_winners "National Rugby League") in their twentieth year of competition. Due to its dramatic ending, the match has been regarded as one of the greatest grand finals in rugby league history, drawing comparisons with the [1989 NSWRL Grand Final](/wiki/1989_NSWRL_season#Grand_final "1989 NSWRL season") and the [1997 ARL Grand Final](/wiki/1997_ARL_season#Grand_Final "1997 ARL season").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Cowboys co-captain [Johnathan Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston"), who kicked the winning field goal for his team, was awarded the [Clive Churchill Medal](/wiki/Clive_Churchill_Medal "Clive Churchill Medal") as the best player on the ground. Pre-match entertainment was headlined by Australian rock band [Cold Chisel](/wiki/Cold_Chisel "Cold Chisel").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Championship game of the National Rugby League season

| 2015 (2015) NRL Grand Final | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| | [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") | [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") | | --- | --- | | 16 | 17 | | |

| [Clive Churchill Medal](/wiki/Clive_Churchill_Medal "Clive Churchill Medal") | [Johnathan Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston") |
| [Australian National anthem](/wiki/Advance_Australia_Fair "Advance Australia Fair") | [Jessica Mauboy](/wiki/Jessica_Mauboy "Jessica Mauboy") |
| [Referee](/wiki/Rugby_league_match_officials#Referee "Rugby league match officials") | [Gerard Sutton](/wiki/Gerard_Sutton_(referee) "Gerard Sutton (referee)") [Ben Cummins](/wiki/Ben_Cummins "Ben Cummins") Brett Suttor (Touch Judge) Steve Carrall (Touch Judge) |
| Attendance | 82,758 |
| Broadcast partners | |
| Broadcasters | * [Nine Network](/wiki/Nine_Network "Nine Network") |
| Commentators | * [Ray Warren](/wiki/Ray_Warren "Ray Warren") [Phil Gould](/wiki/Phil_Gould_(rugby_league) "Phil Gould (rugby league)") [Peter Sterling](/wiki/Peter_Sterling_(rugby_league_commentator) "Peter Sterling (rugby league commentator)") [Wally Lewis](/wiki/Wally_Lewis "Wally Lewis") |
| **[NRL Grand Final](/wiki/NRL_Grand_Final "NRL Grand Final")**  ← [2014](/wiki/2014_NRL_Grand_Final "2014 NRL Grand Final")  [2016](/wiki/2016_NRL_Grand_Final "2016 NRL Grand Final") → | |

The **2015 NRL Grand Final** was the conclusive and premiership-deciding game of the [2015 NRL season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season") and was played on Sunday 4 October at Sydney's [ANZ Stadium](/wiki/Stadium_Australia "Stadium Australia") between the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") and [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").[[1]](#cite_note-Australian_Broadcasting_Corporation_-_4_October_2015_-_NRL_Grand_Final:_North_Queensland_Cowboys_beat_Brisbane_Broncos_in_golden-point_thriller-1) North Queensland won the match 17–16 in [golden point](/wiki/Golden_point "Golden point") extra time, claiming their first [premiership title](/wiki/National_Rugby_League#Title_winners "National Rugby League") in their twentieth year of competition. Due to its dramatic ending, the match has been regarded as one of the greatest grand finals in rugby league history, drawing comparisons with the [1989 NSWRL Grand Final](/wiki/1989_NSWRL_season#Grand_final "1989 NSWRL season") and the [1997 ARL Grand Final](/wiki/1997_ARL_season#Grand_Final "1997 ARL season").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Cowboys co-captain [Johnathan Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston"), who kicked the winning field goal for his team, was awarded the [Clive Churchill Medal](/wiki/Clive_Churchill_Medal "Clive Churchill Medal") as the best player on the ground. Pre-match entertainment was headlined by Australian rock band [Cold Chisel](/wiki/Cold_Chisel "Cold Chisel").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

The [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") qualified for their first grand final appearance since 2006, and had never previously been defeated in a grand final, winning all six they had featured in – 1992, 1993, 1998, 2000, 2006, and 1997 for Super League. It was Brisbane coach [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)")'s ninth involvement in a grand final after coaching the club in all of their previous grand final appearances, as well as co-coaching the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders") to their maiden grand final in [1987](/wiki/1987_NSWRL_Grand_Final "1987 NSWRL Grand Final") and winning a premiership with the [St George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons") in [2010](/wiki/2010_NRL_Grand_Final "2010 NRL Grand Final"). The [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") reached the premiership deciding game for the second time since their inception into the competition in 1995, having lost to the [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers") in their first appearance in [2005](/wiki/2005_NRL_Grand_Final "2005 NRL Grand Final"). It was the second time in history that no New South Wales team featured in the grand final, and the first time that both clubs were Queensland-based. It was the first time that two clubs were [captained](/wiki/Captain_(sports) "Captain (sports)") by [Indigenous Australians](/wiki/Indigenous_Australians "Indigenous Australians") played in an NRL grand final.[[4]](#cite_note-Brisbane_Times_-_30_September_2015_-_Indigenous_captains_Johnathan_Thurston_and_Justin_Hodges_create_grand_final_history-4)[[5]](#cite_note-The_Sydney_Morning_Herald_-_10_June_2016_-Cody_Walker_proud_to_be_among_highest_Indigenous_representation_in_an_NRL_game-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/State_of_Origin_Game_II_2018.jpg/250px-State_of_Origin_Game_II_2018.jpg)](/wiki/File:State_of_Origin_Game_II_2018.jpg)

ANZ Stadium, where the match was played

### 2015 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 2015 season")]

Brisbane, who finished second in the regular season ladder, and North Queensland, who finished third, faced each other during the first week of the finals series in a qualification game, with the Brisbane side prevailing 16-12 in a tight contest at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium") to earn a week off and the right to host a grand final qualifier. Brisbane defeated minor premiers the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") 31-12 in the preliminary final at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium") to claim the first spot in the grand final. In losing to the Brisbane side, North Queensland were forced to play an extra week in the finals and faced the sixth-placed [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") at their home venue of [1300SMILES Stadium](/wiki/1300SMILES_Stadium "1300SMILES Stadium"), beating them convincingly 39-0. They then qualified for the grand final after defeating the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") 32-12 in their preliminary final at [AAMI Park](/wiki/AAMI_Park "AAMI Park").

## Match summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Match summary")]

2015 NRL Grand Final

|  |
| --- |

| [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") | 16 – 17 ([g.p.](/wiki/Golden_point#Rugby_league "Golden point")) | [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Tries:** **2** [Oates](/wiki/Corey_Oates "Corey Oates") rugby ball 7' [Reed](/wiki/Jack_Reed_(rugby_league) "Jack Reed (rugby league)") rugby ball 34' **Goals:** **4** [Parker](/wiki/Corey_Parker_(rugby_league) "Corey Parker (rugby league)") rugby goalposts icon pen 6', 9' (2/2) [Kahu](/wiki/Jordan_Kahu "Jordan Kahu") rugby goalposts icon 35', pen 43' (2/2) | 1st: 14–12 2nd: 2–4 [GP](/wiki/Golden_point "Golden point"): 0–1 [Report](https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/10/04/thurston-kicks-cowboys-to-premiership/) | **Tries:** **3** [O'Neill](/wiki/Justin_O%27Neill "Justin O'Neill") rugby ball 10' [Tamou](/wiki/James_Tamou "James Tamou") rugby ball 25' [Feldt](/wiki/Kyle_Feldt "Kyle Feldt") rugby ball 80' **Goals:** **2** [Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston") rugby goalposts icon 12', 26' (2/3) **Field goals:** **1** [Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston") rugby goalposts icon 83' |

|  |
| --- |
| [ANZ Stadium](/wiki/Stadium_Australia "Stadium Australia"), [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") Attendance: 82,758 Referees: [Ben Cummins](/wiki/Ben_Cummins "Ben Cummins"), [Gerard Sutton](/wiki/Gerard_Sutton_(referee) "Gerard Sutton (referee)") Touch judges: Steve Carrall, Brent Suttor **[Clive Churchill Medal](/wiki/Clive_Churchill_Medal "Clive Churchill Medal"):** [Johnathan Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston") (North Queensland) |

### 1st half

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 1st half")]

After an even opening five minutes, the first points of the game were scored by the Brisbane side in the sixth minute, when a North Queensland handling error in midfield followed shortly by a penalty allowed [Corey Parker](/wiki/Corey_Parker_(rugby_league) "Corey Parker (rugby league)") to kick a 40-metre penalty goal.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) The Brisbane side then returned the first tackle after the restart for the opening try of the game, after a late offload on his own ten-metre line from [Adam Blair](/wiki/Adam_Blair "Adam Blair") to [Anthony Milford](/wiki/Anthony_Milford "Anthony Milford") allowed Milford to set up a line-break down the left wing, culminating in a try to [Corey Oates](/wiki/Corey_Oates "Corey Oates"); Parker converted the try and Brisbane led 8–0.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) The Broncos then knocked on shortly after the restart, and North Queensland scored its opening try from the ensuing scrum set piece, with [Jake Granville](/wiki/Jake_Granville "Jake Granville") breaking the line to set up [Justin O'Neill](/wiki/Justin_O%27Neill "Justin O'Neill") for the try; [Johnathan Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston") converted and Brisbane led 8–6.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) North Queensland nearly scored again three minutes later, with Oates making a try-saving tackle on [Kyle Feldt](/wiki/Kyle_Feldt "Kyle Feldt") after a pass from [Lachlan Coote](/wiki/Lachlan_Coote "Lachlan Coote").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Over the ensuing fifteen minutes, North Queensland had the better of possession and field position. In the 24th minute, attacking from a repeat set which had followed a grubber kick, [James Tamou](/wiki/James_Tamou "James Tamou") took the first pass out of dummy half and scored under the posts; Thurston converted the try to give North Queensland a 12–8 lead.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) Brisbane scored an opportunistic try in the 34th minute, when Thurston lost the ball in a tackle in North Queensland territory, and [Matt Gillett](/wiki/Matt_Gillett "Matt Gillett") recovered for Brisbane and broke the line to put [Jack Reed](/wiki/Jack_Reed_(rugby_league) "Jack Reed (rugby league)") over for a try; [Jordan Kahu](/wiki/Jordan_Kahu "Jordan Kahu") converted and Brisbane regained a 14–12 lead which it took to half time.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6)

### 2nd half

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2nd half")]

Jordan Kahu kicked a penalty goal in the 43rd minute to extend Brisbane's lead to 16–12.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) From that point, the game played out as a defensive battle in Brisbane territory. North Queensland had the better of possession and field position, and generated several repeat sets in attack, but were unable to score due to a combination of poor execution and Brisbane's stout goal line defence. Brisbane actively sought to defend its lead, kicking for touch on the sixth tackle throughout most of the second half to wind down the clock and to slow the game down to manage fatigue.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) North Queensland's two closest opportunities came in the 61st minute, when [Kane Linnett](/wiki/Kane_Linnett "Kane Linnett") dropped a cut-out pass from Thurston which would have seen him score out wide;[[8]](#cite_note-8) and in the 64th minute, when fullback Coote was tackled around the legs by Gillett just short of the try line – Coote reached out to put the ball over the line and was penalised for a double movement.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6)

With four minutes remaining, and the ball deep in North Queensland's territory, [Ben Hunt](/wiki/Ben_Hunt_(rugby_league) "Ben Hunt (rugby league)") conceded a penalty for a dangerous lifting tackle on Linnett, giving North Queensland field position. North Queensland failed to score on the ensuing set; but on Brisbane's following set, Brisbane failed to get to a sixth tackle kick away, after Feldt stripped the ball one-on-one from Hunt at midfield on fourth tackle with one minute remaining.[[9]](#cite_note-hunt-9) North Queensland attacked, and on the sixth tackle, after a couple of powerful runs from forwards Taumalolo, Tamou and Scott, Thurston received the ball on the left edge, beat a couple of tackles, looked left before floating a pass right to link up with his five-eighth [Michael Morgan](/wiki/Michael_Morgan_(rugby_league_born_1991) "Michael Morgan (rugby league born 1991)") who ran diagonally right, cut in to draw three Brisbane defenders then as the full time siren sounded, 5 metres out flicked a short outside pass to winger Feldt whom Oates left unmarked providing him the opportunity to score just inside the right corner post to level the match up at 16-16.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6) Jonathon Thurston had a conversion attempt from the right touchline after full time to win the match. He took a long time to compose himself for the kick, however the ball failed to break enough to the left, hitting the right upright half way up and bouncing out, leaving the game tied up at 16–16 and heading into golden point extra time to break the deadlock.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6)

### Extra time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Extra time")]

North Queensland kicked off first in extra time, and the opening kick-off was knocked on by Ben Hunt, immediately giving North Queensland an attacking scrum on Brisbane's ten-metre line.[[9]](#cite_note-hunt-9) North Queensland set up for the game-winning [field goal](/wiki/Drop_goal "Drop goal"), and after aborting attempts on the first and third tackles due to strong defensive pressure from the Broncos, Johnathan Thurston was able to make up for his missed conversion by slotting the field goal on the fourth tackle from 20 metres out to win the game 17–16. It was the first time an NRL Grand Final had been decided in Golden Point.[[6]](#cite_note-NRL-6)

### Post-match

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Post-match")]

At the post-match ceremony, Johnathan Thurston received the Clive Churchill medal as best on ground. This was then followed by the championship ring ceremony and the co-captains holding the Provan-Summons trophy.

Thurston drew praise for his post-match conduct, notably consoling Ben Hunt.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Many consider the image of Thurston hugging his daughter to be the 'best image' of the grand final.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Aftermath

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_NRL_Grand_Final&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Aftermath")]

Main article: [2016 World Club Series](/wiki/2016_World_Club_Series "2016 World Club Series")

Most commentators, analysts and fans rate the 2015 Grand Final as one of, if not the greatest ever played, ranking it alongside the 1989 decider.

Despite this, the golden point conclusion after Hunt's opening error was considered dissatisfying, and it was a key reason for a change of rules made the following year. Since 2016, tied finals have instead been decided by 10 minutes of extra time (five minutes each way) played in full, with golden point used only if still level after 90 minutes.[[12]](#cite_note-smh1-12)

Both grand finalists, North Queensland and Brisbane, as well as minor premiers the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters"), qualified for the second [World Club Series](/wiki/World_Club_Series "World Club Series"), played in the 2016 pre-season against clubs from the [Super League](/wiki/Super_League "Super League").[[13]](#cite_note-13)
